message:
feat(web/server): tick 167 run_web_server_with_cx + cx-aware shutdown wait (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Eighteenth post-storage tick. web/server.rs had
`start_web_server_with_cx` already shipped but
`run_web_server` (the orchestrator-level entry) was still
legacy-only.

New siblings:
- run_web_server_with_cx — routes the initial bind through
  `start_web_server_with_cx` so the bind phase honours caller
  cancellation, and passes the caller's cx into
  `wait_for_shutdown_signal_with_cx`. A caller-initiated
  cx-cancel now triggers the same graceful-shutdown path
  (`signal_shutdown` + `poke_listener` + drain join handle)
  that a SIGTERM would.

- wait_for_shutdown_signal_with_cx — races the OS signal futures
  against cx cancellation via a 100ms-poll `cancel_fut` inside
  the `select!`. On cx-cancel the function returns `Ok(())` —
  semantically "external shutdown request", indistinguishable
  from a SIGTERM for the caller's graceful-shutdown logic.
  Whichever of (SIGINT, SIGTERM, cx-cancel) fires first wins.

Both unix and non-unix builds have the cx-first variant:
- unix: 3-way select (ctrl_c, SIGTERM, cancel_fut)
- non-unix: 2-way select (ctrl_c, cancel_fut); ctrl_c result
  surfaces a Ctrl+C handler error on the non-cancel path.

Legacy `run_web_server` + `wait_for_shutdown_signal` preserved
unchanged for callers on the ambient-cx path.

Verified via fork bypass: 124/124 web:: tests pass, including
the existing web framework + extractor regression gates.

web/server.rs cx-first coverage: 3/3 entry-level async fns
(start, run, wait_for_shutdown).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
